─[ Lore V1 Go-Live ]─ ┐

Lore V1 Go-Live

Goal

Ship Lore v1 with three-platform support (Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode), then launch with honest positioning and real evidence.

Phases

Phase 0: Identity and Trust Baseline

Status: Complete

GitHub org, domains, licensing, governance, security baseline, v0.1.0 tagged and released via create-lore@0.1.3.

Phase 1: Framework Hardening

Status: Complete (v0.2.0)

Address reliability and trust gaps before public launch.

  • Dynamic nav generation with auto-scaffolding, curated section ordering
  • Work-as-home nav — work subsections under Home tab, no standalone Work tab
  • Agent rules extraction — docs/context/agent-rules.md as sticky PROJECT context
  • Guides rework — commands reference, customization guide, hub-spoke architecture
  • Fix protect-memory.js — narrowed to only block project-root MEMORY.md
  • Narrow Docker volume mount — docs/ and mkdocs.yml only
  • Community health files — CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, SECURITY.md
  • Knowledge map in session banner — directory-only tree
  • Tests for hooks and scripts — 24 tests across generate-nav and session-init
  • Versioning and upgrade path — .lore-config, sync-framework.sh, /lore-update, /lore-status
  • Rename personal/ → local/ for clarity
  • Conventions as directory with per-domain pages, injected via session hook
  • Docs sweep — lore-docs terminology (environment→context), strikethrough standardization

Plans:

  • Adoption Experience and Compatibility (archived)
  • Community and Release Governance (archived)

Phase 2: OpenCode Support + Cleanup

Status: Complete (v0.3.0)

Added OpenCode as a second supported platform via shared lib architecture. Core logic lives in lib/ (CJS), with thin adapters for each platform.

Design decisions:

  • CLAUDE.md, not AGENTS.md — OpenCode’s opencode.json points to ["CLAUDE.md", "MEMORY.local.md"]. One canonical instructions file serves both platforms.
  • Shared lib architecture — lib/banner.js, lib/tracker.js, lib/memory-guard.js contain core logic. createRequire(import.meta.url) bridges ESM plugins to CJS lib.
  • Aligned filenames — both platforms use session-init, knowledge-tracker, protect-memory.
  • No delegation-enforcer — requires project-specific hardcoded mappings. Knowledge-tracker provides soft reminders instead.
  • Template ships both platforms — every Lore project gets hooks/ and .opencode/. No installer flags.

Deliverables:

  • Shared lib: lib/banner.js, lib/tracker.js, lib/memory-guard.js
  • OpenCode plugins: three ESM adapters in .opencode/plugins/
  • opencode.json config in template
  • Claude Code hooks refactored to thin CJS adapters
  • Aligned filenames across both platforms (knowledge-tracker not post-edit-reminder)
  • Configurable escalation thresholds via .lore-config
  • README honesty pass — two-platform quick-start, before/after, supported platforms table
  • 69 lore tests + 8 create-lore tests passing
  • Published: lore v0.3.0, create-lore@0.3.0 on npm

Plan: OpenCode Support (archived)

Phase 3: Cursor + Platform Maturity

Status: Complete (v0.4.0 → v0.5.0)

Added Cursor as a third platform, introduced cross-repo linking, restructured knowledge architecture, and closed feature parity gaps. Two releases in this phase.

v0.4.0 — Cursor Support + Cross-Repo Linking:

  • Cursor hooks: sessionStart, beforeSubmitPrompt, beforeReadFile, afterFileEdit, afterShellExecution
  • .cursor/hooks.json config, .cursor/hooks/ adapters, .cursor/rules/ rules
  • Vendor-agnostic instructions — .lore/instructions.md as canonical source
  • Platform-neutral skills and agents — .lore/ as canonical, platform copies generated
  • lore-link — hub-spoke cross-repo hook generation for all three platforms
  • sync-framework.sh integrates lore-link refresh
  • CI workflow and release config

v0.5.0 — Standardization + Knowledge Architecture:

  • lore- prefix on all user-invocable skills
  • LORE_DEBUG mode for hook diagnostics
  • Configurable treeDepth via .lore-config
  • Split docs/context/ into docs/context/ (rules) + docs/knowledge/ (reference)
  • Broader environment subdirs: inventory/, decisions/, reference/, diagrams/
  • Nav reorder: Work → Knowledge → Context → Guides
  • OpenCode context-path-guide plugin (feature parity with Claude Code)
  • Cursor condensed banner on every prompt (compaction resilience)
  • Cursor .plans/ routing rule
  • Archives visible in nav, archive cleanup in /lore-consolidate
  • 156 lore tests passing (up from 69)

Three-platform hook coverage (as shipped):

BehaviorClaude CodeCursorOpenCode
Session bannersession-init.jssession-init.jssession-init.js
Per-prompt reminderprompt-preamble.jsprompt-preamble.jssession-init.js (experimental.chat.system.transform)
MEMORY.md guardprotect-memory.jsprotect-memory.js (reads only)protect-memory.js
Knowledge captureknowledge-tracker.jsknowledge-tracker.jsknowledge-tracker.js
Context path guidecontext-path-guide.js—context-path-guide.js

Phase 4: Launch Content and Distribution

Status: Active

Close pre-launch gates, build proof assets, and execute staged distribution. Single consolidated plan — absorbs remaining items from archived Adoption Experience and Community Governance plans.

Priority refactor: Phase 4 is now evidence-first. We do not optimize channel content before proving reliability and value with measurable outcomes.

  • P0: Proof assets — before/after examples, workflow references, evidence snapshot (2026-02-23: Full A/B test complete. N=10 all conditions (50 sessions). Runbook steady state 59% cheaper than raw Claude ($0.19 vs $0.45 median). Published: cost-evidence on lore-docs, lore-gotcha-demo public test repo. Brainstorm: docs/workflow/brainstorms/cold-warm-ab-test/)
  • P0: Reliability and context-cost evidence — hook token cost analysis and platform behavior verification (Context injection audit complete — 7,800 tokens at session start, 96 tokens/turn preamble. Ambiguity scan added to reduce peripheral reasoning degradation.)
  • P1: Cross-platform install validation — clean Mac test, Windows WSL documented, preflight check in create-lore (OS, bash version, Node version), graceful degradation without Docker
  • P1: Pre-launch gates (release cadence, re-open checklist, merge expectations)
  • P1: Demo video (3 min) — bootstrap → snag → fieldnote capture → reuse
  • P1: Blog post — lead with cost evidence (“$0.45 → $0.19, proved across 50 sessions”), not architecture
  • P2: Early seeding — low-stakes venues (Claude Code Discord, Reddit comments, not posts), target 10-20 real external users, fix what breaks
  • P2: Channel distribution — r/ClaudeAI → HN Show HN → Twitter/X thread + dev.to same week
  • Foundational positioning assets (README overhaul + messaging kit)
  • v0.10.0 shipped (2026-02-21) — hook profiles, operator.gitignore persistence, framework consolidation into .lore/, lore-docker/lore-delegate skills, threshold-only bash tracking. All 4 repos published (lore, create-lore@npm, lore-docs, lore-docker@DockerHub).
  • v0.11.0 synced (2026-02-22) — new skills (mcp-stdio-content-length-framing, node-macos-stdin-fd), docker search startup delay snag, lore-gotcha-demo public repo
  • v0.12.9 shipped (2026-02-24) — harness rename (framework→harness), first-session setup guides as seeds, all skills synced to .claude/skills/, user-invocable frontmatter required, config-driven model stamping, Docker runtime fixes (panzoom assets, site_url), release.sh tags all 3 repos
  • v0.13.0 shipped (2026-02-24) — platform docs restructure (4-page section with maturity levels), cost evidence transparency (full caveats, contribute section, lore-gotcha-demo expanded), first-session Phase 0 (version control setup), Docker nav link fix (SITE_URL separation), seed runbook example flagging
  • Published raw A/B test data (50 sessions) to lore-gotcha-demo/results/ and lore-docs cost-evidence section (5 pages). Expanded harness engineering narrative with industry references (OpenAI, martinfowler.com, Anthropic, Karpathy).

Plans:

  • Launch Content and Distribution
  • Review Findings Remediation
  • Skill and Agent Ownership (archived)

Phase 5: Public Launch and Iteration

Status: Planned

  • Show HN — lead with cost evidence, Tuesday-Thursday 9-11am ET. Title: cost reduction + “open source”, not feature list
  • Same week: Twitter/X thread (problem → insight → proof → npx create-lore) + dev.to post
  • Triage issues quickly (< 4h setup failures, < 24h feature requests)
  • Convert recurring friction into docs/automation
  • Cut v1.0.0 based on launch feedback

Success Criteria

Phase 1:

  • Hook test coverage exists
  • protect-memory.js doesn’t break Claude Code’s built-in memory
  • Docker volume mount narrowed
  • v0.2.0 tagged and published

Phase 2:

  • OpenCode plugins work end-to-end (session injection, capture, memory protection)
  • create-lore scaffolds both Claude Code and OpenCode files
  • Same knowledge base works from both Claude Code and OpenCode in one repo
  • README reflects real two-platform evidence

Phase 3:

  • All three platforms have hook adapters and fire correctly
  • Cross-repo linking works for all platforms
  • Knowledge architecture split is clean (context/ vs knowledge/)
  • 156+ tests passing across hooks, scripts, and plugins

Phase 4:

  • README converts a 10-second scan into understanding
  • Demo video exists and shows real workflow
  • Blog post is complete and evidence-backed

Phase 5:

  • Soft-launch feedback incorporated
  • v1.0.0 released

Open Questions

  1. Portable skill format — current skill markdown references Claude Code concepts (Task tool, model routing). Skills are surfaced via the session banner’s knowledge map on both platforms, so the content works cross-platform even if some references are Claude-specific. Monitor whether this causes confusion.
  2. Existing project migration — npx create-lore scaffolds fresh repos. lore init for established projects is a future opportunity. Deferred — not needed for v1 launch.
  3. AGENTS.md vs CLAUDE.md — Resolved: OpenCode’s opencode.json points to CLAUDE.md directly. One canonical file, no duplication.

Privacy Constraint

Do not store personal identifiers, personal handles, personal emails, or private legal/account details in tracked files. Repository remains private until launch-readiness gate passes.

PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
